title: wt step prune: worktree pruning and concurrent .git/config safety
summary: `wt step prune` scans worktrees and orphan branches and removes stale ones. As a destructive operation it must require explicit consent for force-removal, must not discard uncommitted or untracked work, and runs pre/post-remove hooks only after the approval gate clears them. Concurrent `git worktree` mutations race on `.git/config` (the Windows prune race, issue #2801) so pruning must tolerate or serialize that. Complements feat_custom_remove, which covers `wt remove` and the picker. See CLAUDE.md > Data Safety.

ownedFiles.reason: prune entrypoint: candidate selection, age checks, removal, hook approval

contextFiles.reason: ApprovedHookPlan: prune is a gate-then-mutate operation
